hook.text: Your phone contains dozens of rare minerals. If supply chains break, entire industries halt overnight.

buildup.text: Strategic minerals are essential for technology and defense but come from few countries. Supply risk drives policy.

discovery.text: Rare earth elements power magnets in wind turbines and electric cars. China produces over 60 percent of the supply.

twist.text: Recycling and substitution can reduce dependence. Urban mining of old electronics recovers valuable metals.

climax.text: Strategic minerals shape global politics. Control over supply chains gives nations leverage over industries.

punchline.text: Rocks beneath the ground drive world politics.

quiz.question: Why are rare earth elements considered strategic minerals?
options: They are the most abundant elements on Earth
They are essential for technology but sourced from few countries
They are only found in outer space
